required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on March 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX for Borderline Resectable Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma with KRAS Mutation

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates neoadjuvant FOLFIRINOX (5-FU, leucovorin, irinotecan, oxaliplatin) in patients with borderline resectable pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) with KRAS mutation. The goal is to achieve sufficient tumor downsizing to allow R0 resection, specifically targeting patients with superior mesenteric artery abutment of less than 180 degrees. CA 19-9 response and rate of R0 resection are co-primary endpoints.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with cytologically or histologically confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma staged as borderline resectable per NCCN criteria, KRAS mutation confirmed by NGS on biopsy specimen, and no distant metastatic disease (M0) are eligible. Biliary decompression with stent placement is required prior to initiation of systemic therapy if bilirubin is elevated. Patients receive FOLFIRINOX every 2 weeks for 4 planned cycles followed by restaging CT with pancreas protocol. Patients who achieve sufficient downsizing proceed to surgical evaluation (Whipple procedure or distal pancreatectomy). CA 19-9 is monitored at baseline and before each cycle as a surrogate marker of response. Patients with mild malnutrition are eligible with nutritional support (pancreatic enzyme replacement and dietary counseling).

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically or cytologically confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC)
- Borderline resectable disease per NCCN criteria: tumor abutment of superior mesenteric artery (SMA) less than 180 degrees or short-segment portal vein involvement
- Clinical stage cT3N0-N1M0 (Stage II-III), no distant metastatic disease
- KRAS mutation confirmed (any codon) by tumor NGS or biopsy-based molecular testing
- Biliary obstruction resolved or managed with biliary stent prior to chemotherapy initiation (total bilirubin less than 2x ULN at enrollment)
- CA 19-9 elevation at diagnosis (any level)
- Age 18 to 75 years
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.5, platelets greater than or equal to 100, creatinine less than or equal to 1.5x ULN, total bilirubin less than or equal to 2x ULN, AST/ALT less than or equal to 2.5x ULN
- Mild to moderate malnutrition permitted with concurrent nutritional support
Exclusion Criteria:
- Metastatic disease (Stage IV): liver metastases, peritoneal carcinomatosis, or distant lymph node involvement beyond the resection field
- Locally advanced unresectable PDAC (SMA abutment greater than or equal to 180 degrees or celiac axis involvement)
- BRCA1 or BRCA2 germline pathogenic mutation (these patients should be directed to PARP inhibitor or platinum-based trials)
- MSI-H tumor (eligible for immunotherapy-based studies)
- Prior chemotherapy or radiation therapy for pancreatic cancer
- Active pancreatitis
- Prior Whipple procedure or distal pancreatectomy
- ECOG performance status 2 or greater
- Active infection or sepsis at time of enrollment
- Severe hepatic dysfunction (Child-Pugh B or C)

facility.name: GI Oncology and Pancreatic Surgery Program
